dataQualityWarning.title: Data Quality Warning
dataQualityWarning.message:
When prices are scattered and confidence is low, it usually means the data is messy or the market's uncertain.

The prices are all over the place (confidence: {confidence}/100) and asking prices are {marketPressure}% vs FMV. This usually means your search is mixing different card types, conditions, or variations together. Try making your search more specific to get better results.

seller: Be careful pricing your card based on outliers.
Buyers will question your price and negotiate aggressively.
Before listing, tighten your search to match your exact card.
flipper: This isn't a reliable market for consistent flips.
Only buy if the deal is obviously mispriced and easy to verify.
Otherwise, the risk outweighs the reward.
collector: This is a "pause and research" moment.
Waiting for cleaner data usually leads to better buying decisions.
No need to rush unless the card is extremely scarce.

twoTierMarket.title: Two-Tier Market Detected
twoTierMarket.message:
This market shows two distinct activity levels by price tier.

Cards priced below FMV show much higher sales activity relative to supply ({absorptionBelow}x absorption: {salesBelow} sales vs {belowFMV} listings), while higher-priced cards show low activity ({absorptionAbove} absorption: {salesAbove} sales vs {aboveFMV} listings). Average asking price is {marketPressure}% vs FMV.

seller: Cards priced near or below fair value show stronger buyer activity.
Higher-priced listings are sitting with little competition for them.
Competitive pricing tends to generate more buyer engagement.
flipper: This is a good flipping setup.
Buy below fair value and sell close to it.
Avoid premium pricing—those listings tend to stall.
collector: A solid time to buy if you stick to fair prices.
Ignore overpriced listings; they don't reflect real demand.
Patient buyers have the advantage here.

highRiskConditions.title: High Risk Market Conditions
highRiskConditions.message:
Prices are high, but sales activity is low

Sellers are asking {marketPressure}% above FMV, but recent sales activity is low relative to available supply (market activity: {liquidityScore}/100). Listings are priced well above what buyers have recently paid. Waiting for sellers to adjust prices, or looking for better-priced alternatives, is worth considering.

seller: This is a tough environment to sell in.
Expect slow sales or price drops.
If you need to sell, pricing below the crowd helps.
flipper: This is usually a bad setup.
High prices plus low demand leave little room for profit.
Better opportunities exist elsewhere.
collector: Waiting often pays off here.
Markets like this tend to cool down.
Patience can lead to better entry prices later.

overpricedActiveMarket.title: Overpriced but Active Market
overpricedActiveMarket.message:
Prices are high, and sales activity remains strong

Asking prices are {marketPressure}% above FMV, and recent sales activity is high relative to supply (market activity: {liquidityScore}/100). Sellers currently have the upper hand. Prices could soften if market activity cools, but for now buyers are engaging at elevated prices.

seller: This is a strong selling window.
Buyers are accepting higher prices right now.
Consider selling — current activity levels favor sellers.
flipper: Flips are possible, but timing matters.
You need to buy and resell quickly.
Miss the timing, and you risk holding overpriced inventory.
collector: You're paying extra to get a card immediately.
If you don't need the card now, waiting is usually safer.
Great cards often come back down once hype fades.

fairPricingLimitedDemand.title: Fair Pricing, Limited Demand
fairPricingLimitedDemand.message:
Prices make sense, but sales activity is low

Prices are fairly reasonable ({marketPressure}% vs FMV), but sales activity is low relative to available supply (market activity: {liquidityScore}/100). This could mean the card is losing popularity, or that supply is outpacing buyer interest. If this is a recent release, it may simply be that more sellers than buyers are active right now.

seller: Slow sales are likely, even at fair prices.
Pricing toward the lower end of the FMV range may attract more activity.
Otherwise, patience is required.
flipper: Not ideal for quick flips.
Even good deals may take time to resell.
Only buy if you expect a future catalyst.
collector: This can be a great quiet buying opportunity.
Fair prices without hype often age well.
Especially attractive for iconic or historically stable cards.

strongBuyOpportunity.title: Strong Buy Opportunity
strongBuyOpportunity.message:
Current sellers are asking {absMarketPressure}% below Fair Market Value

Active listings are priced below what cards recently sold for, and recent sales activity is strong relative to supply (market activity: {liquidityScore}/100). Current asking prices are undervalued compared to recent sales. This could mean sellers haven't adjusted to recent sales trends, or a player is breaking out.

seller: Current asks are below recent sales — you could price at or near Fair Market Value and benefit from the gap.
Pricing toward Fair Market Value is supported by current sales activity.
Consider pricing at Fair Market Value or just above it
flipper: This is an excellent setup.
Buy at current prices before sellers adjust the gap.
Delays matter here—once sellers reprice, margins shrink.
collector: This is one of the better times to buy if you're optimistic about the player's potential
You're entering below fair value in a market with active recent sales.
Acting sooner usually beats waiting in conditions like this.

healthyMarketConditions.title: Healthy Market Conditions
healthyMarketConditions.message:
Fair prices and active buyers

Prices are fair ({marketPressure}% vs FMV) and there's plenty of buyer activity (liquidity: {liquidityScore}/100). This is a healthy, well-functioning market where both buyers and sellers are active. Prices accurately reflect current demand: good conditions for both buying and selling.

seller: Fair pricing is being rewarded with steady sales.
No need to overthink timing—this is a good environment to list.
Well-presented listings should move at reasonable prices.
flipper: Opportunities exist, but they're not automatic.
Profits depend on buying well, not on market imbalance.
Focus on small edges rather than big swings.
collector: A comfortable, low-stress time to buy.
You're unlikely to overpay or miss out by waiting briefly.
Buy based on preference, not fear of price movement.

balancedMarket.title: Balanced Market
balancedMarket.message: Prices are in the middle range ({marketPressure}% vs FMV) with moderate sales activity (market activity: {liquidityScore}/100). This is a normal, stable market: nothing particularly remarkable happening. Use your normal judgment when buying or selling.

bimodalMarketWarning.title: Fragmented Market - Multiple Price Points
bimodalMarketWarning.message:
Cards are selling at very different prices, with a clear activity split by price tier.

This market shows a fragmented pattern: cards below FMV show higher sales activity relative to supply ({absorptionBelow}x absorption: {salesBelow} sales vs {belowFMV} listings), while cards above FMV show much lower activity ({absorptionAbove} absorption: {salesAbove} sales vs {aboveFMV} listings). FMV Reliability is {confidence}/100, confirming price scatter. This suggests distinct buyer segments operating at different price points.

seller: This market rewards strategic pricing.
Cards priced below FMV show stronger buyer engagement.
Premium pricing leads to low activity in fragmented markets.
Consider your urgency when choosing a price tier.
flipper: Target the higher-absorption zone below FMV.
Buy in the value segment, sell at or just below FMV.
Avoid premium pricing — those listings stall in fragmented markets.
Volume beats premium pricing in bimodal conditions.
collector: Two clear buying windows exist in this market.
Value segment offers faster transaction certainty.
Premium listings may negotiate down over time.
Choose your tier based on urgency vs patience.

sections.content: Market Pressure compares what sellers are asking today to what buyers recently paid. It does not affect Fair Market Value.
Note: Outlier prices are filtered using IQR method for accuracy.

items.title: 0% to 15% (HEALTHY)
items.meaning: Normal pricing friction. Sellers price slightly above recent sales to leave room for negotiation.
items.action: Fair pricing - safe to buy at asking prices or make small offers.

items.title: 15% to 30% (OPTIMISTIC)
items.meaning: Seller optimism. Prices drifting above recent buyer behavior.
items.action: Make offers 10-20% below asking - sellers are likely open to negotiation.

items.title: 30% to 50% (RESISTANCE)
items.meaning: Overpriced market. Clear resistance between buyers and sellers.
items.action: Be patient. Sellers will likely need to lower prices or accept significantly lower offers (20-30% below ask).

items.title: 50%+ (EXTREME)
items.meaning: Extremely high asking prices. Listings unlikely to transact near current levels unless demand and liquidity is also high.
items.action: Wait for price corrections or look for better-priced alternatives. These sellers are detached from market reality. Also check liquidity score.

items.title: Negative % (BELOW FMV)
items.meaning: Sellers are asking less than recent sale prices.
items.action: These may be undervalued or motivated sellers. Do your due diligence before committing to a sale or purchase.

sections.content: Market Pressure above 30% suggests waiting for price corrections or making significantly lower offers. Below 0% indicates potential buying opportunities if buyer demand is healthy.
If cards recently sold for $100 (FMV), but current listings ask $140, that's +40% Market Pressure (Resistance) = sellers are asking too much.

items: Low: Less than 5 active listings (use with caution)

sections.content: Market Confidence measures how consistent prices are in the market. Higher consistency = more reliable data and clearer pricing signals.

items.title: 85-100 (EXCELLENT CONSENSUS)
items.meaning: Exceptional price consistency with tight clustering - excellent market consensus on value.
items.action: FMV estimates are highly reliable. Safe to use for pricing decisions.

items.title: 70-84 (GOOD CONSENSUS)
items.meaning: Solid price consistency - market has good agreement on value.
items.action: FMV estimates are reliable. Good for most pricing decisions.

items.title: 55-69 (MODERATE VARIATION)
items.meaning: Noticeable price variation but overall market is functional.
items.action: FMV estimates are reasonably reliable. Consider using price ranges and watching for trends.

items.title: 40-54 (HIGH VARIATION)
items.meaning: High price variation - market shows significant uncertainty.
items.action: Use caution with FMV estimates. Consider refining search terms or gathering more data.

items.title: 25-39 (VERY HIGH VARIATION)
items.meaning: Very high price variation - unreliable market signals.
items.action: FMV estimates may not be accurate. Refine search or check for data quality issues.

items.title: 0-24 (MARKET CHAOS)
items.meaning: Extreme price scatter - no market consensus.
items.action: Data is unreliable. Check for miscategorized listings or search errors.

sections.content: Market Confidence tells you how reliable the data is, not what the value is. High confidence means prices are clustered together. Low confidence means prices are scattered and unpredictable.
If 20 cards sold between $95-$105 (tight range), confidence is HIGH (80+). If they sold between $50-$200 (wide range), confidence is LOW (30 or less).

items: Make search terms more specific (exact card number, parallel type)
Filter out unrelated variations (use "Base Only" or exclude parallels)
Check for grading consistency (don't mix raw with graded)

sections.content: Market Activity measures how much recent sales activity exists relative to current supply. It describes the market landscape — not how fast your specific listing will sell.
Recent Sales (decay-weighted) / Active Buy It Now Listings
A ratio above 1.0 means recent sales outpace current supply. Below 1.0 means more supply than recent sales activity.

items.title: Ratio ≥ 1.0 (BUYERS ARE ACTIVE)
items.meaning: Recent sales are outpacing active listings — demand is strong relative to current supply.
items.action: Healthy market conditions for sellers. Competitive pricing is rewarded.

items.title: Ratio 0.5–1.0 (MODERATE ACTIVITY)
items.meaning: Balanced market — sales activity and supply are reasonably matched.
items.action: Normal market conditions. No strong signal in either direction.

items.title: Ratio 0.2–0.5 (MORE SELLERS THAN BUYERS)
items.meaning: Active listings outnumber recent sales — buyers have plenty of options.
items.action: A crowded market. Pricing toward the lower end of the FMV range may attract more attention.

items.title: Ratio < 0.2 (FEW ACTIVE BUYERS)
items.meaning: Very few recent sales relative to current supply — a thin market.
items.action: Proceed cautiously. Both the FMV and the selling environment carry more uncertainty here.

sections.content: Market Activity does NOT predict how fast your listing will sell. Sell speed depends on your ask price, listing quality, and timing — none of which this metric can measure. It tells you about the market environment, not your outcome.

items: More reliable: 10+ recent sales AND 10+ active listings
